mechanics.telegraph: All three bosses simultaneously activate Danger Zones; one specific boss also has a defiance bar.
mechanics.effect_on_fail: Wrong bar broken — no safe zone created — squad wipes in the red Danger Zone.
mechanics.effect_on_success: Correct bar broken; safe zone confirmed; squad survives and continues.
mechanics.handling: Puzzle 1 (80%): Break Arsenite's bar, safe zone around Vermilion. Puzzle 2 (40%): Break Vermilion's bar, safe zone behind Indigo. Puzzle 3 (10%): Break Indigo's bar, safe zone next to Vermilion. Memorize the sequence: Arsenite → Vermilion → Indigo.

role_tips.dps: Focus all CC on the correct bar boss. Do not hit other bars.
role_tips.support: Call out which bar to break before each puzzle phase.
role_tips.heal: Move to safe zone first, then use CC if you have it.
role_tips.tank: Assign bar-breaking responsibility before the fight begins.

mechanics.name: Power Transfer (Phase 3)
mechanics.description: In phase 3 when two bosses are active, if they come too close together a blue tether forms between them and the shielded boss gains access to its full attack arsenal (Power Transfer). A brown tether warns of close proximity before the blue tether activates.
mechanics.trigger: Two active bosses approach each other — brown warning tether, then blue Power Transfer tether.
mechanics.telegraph: Brown tether (warning, no Power Transfer yet); blue tether (Power Transfer active).
mechanics.effect_on_fail: Shielded boss uses its full attack rotation, dramatically increasing incoming damage.
mechanics.effect_on_success: Bosses are kept separated; shielded boss only uses auto-attacks.
mechanics.handling: In phase 3, always maintain separation between the two active bosses. Fixation holders must actively lead bosses to opposite sides of the arena. Prioritize survival over perfect positioning if separation becomes difficult.

role_tips.dps: If you notice a brown tether, call it so Fixation holders can separate the bosses.
role_tips.support: As Fixation holder, actively walk your boss away from the other active boss.
role_tips.heal: If Power Transfer activates, use emergency cooldowns — the damage spike is severe.
role_tips.tank: Immediately separate bosses on any tether — do not wait for the blue tether.

timeline_notes: Phase 1: Learn Danger Zone safe spots. Control Fixation. Place Boiling Aether at the edge.
Puzzle 1 (80%): All Danger Zones fire — break Arsenite's bar, safe zone at Vermilion.
Phase 2: Inactive bosses add Danger Zone overlap. Two sets of red zones can appear.
Puzzle 2 (40%): Break Vermilion's bar, safe zone behind Indigo.
Phase 3: Two bosses active. Keep them separated — brown then blue tether means Power Transfer.
Puzzle 3 (10%): Break Indigo's bar, safe zone next to Vermilion.
Phase 4: All three active, no shielding — burn to zero.

pre_pull: Bosses fight one at a time in order: Vermilion first, then Arsenite, then Indigo — repeat.
Only the current active boss takes damage — others are shielded.
Danger Zones: move to white immediately. Do not stand in red when it detonates.
Puzzle phases: break the correct bar. Puzzle 1 — Arsenite's bar. Puzzle 2 — Vermilion's bar. Puzzle 3 — Indigo's bar.
Boiling Aether puddles go to the arena edge — always. Fixation holders lead bosses to the edge.
Dual Horizon on Vermilion: read your own white arrows — do not copy the person next to you.
Phase 3 — two bosses active. Keep them separated. Brown tether = warning, blue tether = Power Transfer.
phase_walkthrough: Phase 1 (100-80%): Vermilion, then Arsenite, then Indigo. One active at a time. Dodge Danger Zones.
Puzzle 1 (80%): All three Danger Zones fire — break Arsenite's bar, safe zone at Vermilion.
Phase 2 (80-40%): Same order, but inactive bosses add extra Danger Zone helpers.
Puzzle 2 (40%): Break Vermilion's bar, safe zone behind Indigo.
Phase 3 (40-10%): Two active bosses. Keep separated. Vulnerable target only.
Puzzle 3 (10%): Break Indigo's bar, safe zone next to Vermilion.
Phase 4 (10-0%): All three active, no shielding — full burn.
recovery_plans: If a wrong bar is broken during a puzzle phase, the squad will wipe — accept it and reinforce bar assignments on the next pull.
If Power Transfer activates in phase 3, use all defensive cooldowns and immediately separate the bosses.
If Boiling Aether puddles fill the center, find the cleanest remaining ground and tighten boss positioning to stop adding more puddles.

common_mistakes: Breaking the wrong defiance bar during a puzzle phase, causing an instant squad wipe.
Copying a nearby player's position for Dual Horizon instead of reading your own white arrows.
Allowing Boiling Aether puddles to accumulate in the arena center, cutting off the DPS position.
Not controlling Fixation, causing bosses to wander and drop puddles in the worst locations.
Allowing two active bosses to drift together in phase 3, triggering Power Transfer.
callouts: Danger Zone — move to white!
Puzzle — break Arsenite's bar at 80%!
Puzzle — break Vermilion's bar at 40%!
Puzzle — break Indigo's bar at 10%!
Dual Horizon — read your own arrows!
Puddles to the edge — move the boss!
Brown tether — separate the bosses now!

name: Cosmic Observatory

overview.summary: A fast-paced encounter against Dagda, a jotun wizard with high health and quick-startup attacks. Dagda uses a scripted attack rotation with mechanics that expand as health depletes. The critical fight-defining mechanics are Shooting Stars (body-blocking), Soul Feast (Special Action Skill), and Planet Crash (defiance bar that must always be broken).
overview.goal: Defeat Dagda by body-blocking Shooting Stars, destroying Soul Feast with the Purifying Light special action, breaking Planet Crash's defiance bar, and clearing add splits quickly.
wipe_conditions: Shooting Stars fires on the target with fewer than 5 players blocking — target takes 90% max HP damage and the squad gains Infirm.
Planet Crash defiance bar is not broken — all players take 90% max HP unavoidable damage.
Players are slow to exit during intermission split phases and stand in the lethal red pool.
Demonic Fever circles overlap and instantly down multiple players.
soft_enrage: Demonic Blast debuff stacks accumulate — at 10 stacks, affected players treat nearby squadmates as enemies.
overview.hard_enrage: No hard enrage. Time of day is locked at instance creation.

phases.name: Phase 1 – 100% to 75%
phases.description: Dagda uses Shooting Stars and Spinning Nebula in rotation. At 90% health, Spreads or Soul Feast are added after Spinning Nebula.

phases.name: Intermission 1 – Split Phase (75%)
phases.description: Dagda becomes untargetable, pushes players away, channels Demonic Blast (stand in safe zones between the cones), and spawns a large lethal red pool. Kill 2 Kryptis minions to make Dagda targetable again. Spread mechanic can continue during the transition.

phases.name: Phase 2 – 75% to 50%
phases.description: Soul Feast becomes available below 75%. Added after Spinning Nebula when Zojja screams.

phases.name: Intermission 2 – Split Phase (50%)
phases.description: Same as intermission 1 but with 4 Kryptis minions to kill.

phases.name: Phase 3 – 50% to 25%
phases.description: Below 50%, Rain of Comets and Planet Crash (defiance bar) become available in addition to all prior mechanics.

phases.name: Intermission 3 – Split Phase (25%)
phases.description: Same as prior intermissions but with 6 Kryptis minions to kill.

phases.name: Phase 4 – 25% to 0%
phases.description: All mechanics active. Planet Crash defiance bar remains a kill requirement. Burn Dagda to zero.

roles.dps: Body-block for Shooting Stars if you are not the target. Spread immediately during Demonic Fever. Pick up white orbs during Soul Feast and use the Purifying Light special action on the Soul Feast entity. Break the Planet Crash defiance bar.
roles.support: Provide projectile mitigation for Spinning Nebula. Maintain boons during intermission splits. Help clear Kryptis adds quickly.
roles.heal: Manage sustained spread damage and Demonic Blast hits. Be ready for emergencies if Planet Crash fires.
roles.tank: No traditional tank. Dagda targets randomly. Ensure all players are positioned in a cluster for body-blocking efficiency.

mechanics.name: Shooting Stars
mechanics.description: Dagda selects a random target. Red text appears: 'Beware the Shooting Stars. Hide behind your allies!' At least 5 squad members must position between Dagda and the target to body-block the attack. If fewer than 5 block, the target takes 90% max HP damage and the squad gains Infirm (50% max HP damage).
mechanics.trigger: Appears regularly throughout the fight — Dagda's primary repeating mechanic.
mechanics.telegraph: Red warning text on screen; target symbol appears above the targeted player; green arrow indicates the block direction.
mechanics.effect_on_fail: Target takes near-lethal damage; squad gains Infirm; significantly increases difficulty of subsequent mechanics.
mechanics.effect_on_success: The attack is blocked; target takes no damage; no Infirm applied.
mechanics.handling: If you are the target, move away from the group to allow blockers to line up cleanly. All other players position between Dagda and the target. At minimum 5 players must be in the blocking line.

role_tips.dps: If not the target, immediately position between Dagda and the target. Do not stand still while attacking.
role_tips.support: Move to block — boons can wait 2 seconds.
role_tips.heal: Block first. Heal after.
role_tips.tank: Call 'block up' and move to the blocking position immediately.

mechanics.name: Spinning Nebula
mechanics.description: Dagda spins with his greatsword and fires destructible or reflectable orbs dealing high damage. He may teleport away before spinning, in which case orbs aim only at the center.
mechanics.trigger: Second mechanic in Dagda's scripted rotation.
mechanics.telegraph: Dagda winds up a spin; orbs are visible in flight.
mechanics.effect_on_fail: Players take heavy damage from multiple orb hits, potentially causing downs without strong healing.
mechanics.effect_on_success: Players back away or dodge immediately; projectile destruction or reflects neutralize orbs.
mechanics.handling: Back away from Dagda or dodge immediately when the spin begins. Projectile reflect or destruction abilities can neutralize the orbs.

role_tips.dps: Dodge back when Dagda begins spinning.
role_tips.support: Use projectile mitigation for the orb volley.
role_tips.heal: Be ready for spikes — Spinning Nebula is high damage without mitigation.
role_tips.tank: Call the spin so the group backs away.

mechanics.name: Demonic Fever (Spread)
mechanics.description: Large expanding AoEs appear centered on 7 random players. Two or more overlapping circles instantly down players inside them.
mechanics.trigger: Occasional throughout phases; can coincide with intermission transitions.
mechanics.telegraph: Large expanding circles appear on affected players.
mechanics.effect_on_fail: Players cluster — circles overlap and instant-down multiple players.
mechanics.effect_on_success: Players spread to separate positions and hold still; circles detonate without overlap.
mechanics.handling: Pick a spot and move immediately. Hold position once separated. Ranged and support players move further out; melee stay closer to avoid being forced to the arena edge where movement is limited.

role_tips.dps: Pick a direction and commit to it. Do not bunch near others with circles.
role_tips.support: Spread away from the group. Swiftness helps everyone find separate spots quickly.
role_tips.heal: Spread. Do not stand near anyone else with a circle.
role_tips.tank: Call 'spread' the instant circles appear.

mechanics.name: Demonic Blast (Pizza Cones)
mechanics.description: Eight cone-shaped AoEs radiate from Dagda with a long delay before detonating. Detonation applies 3 stacks of a debuff that becomes dangerous at 10 stacks (players treat nearby squadmates as enemies).
mechanics.trigger: Channeled during all intermission split phases.
mechanics.telegraph: Eight pizza-slice cone indicators radiating from Dagda.
mechanics.effect_on_fail: Players stand in cones and accumulate the debuff. At 10 stacks they attack allies.
mechanics.effect_on_success: Players stand between cones in safe zones and take no hit.
mechanics.handling: Stand between two adjacent cones. Dodge toward a safe zone if caught in a cone.

role_tips.dps: Find a gap between cones and stand in it.
role_tips.support: Same — stand in a safe gap.
role_tips.heal: Position between cones immediately when intermission begins.
role_tips.tank: Call out the Demonic Blast cones as a reminder to find gaps.

mechanics.name: Soul Feast (Special Action Skill)
mechanics.description: A targetable Soul Feast entity spawns with 2 white orbs nearby. It applies a debuff and Infirm and fixates on a random player with the text 'You are targeted by Soul Feast. Run!' A player must pick up a white orb to gain the Purifying Light special action skill, then target the Soul Feast entity and activate the skill to destroy it.
mechanics.trigger: Spawns after Spinning Nebula below 75% health, triggered by Zojja's scream.
mechanics.telegraph: Soul Feast entity spawns; target text appears on the fixated player.
mechanics.effect_on_fail: Soul Feast continues applying Infirm debuff. If not destroyed, it persists and debilitates the group.
mechanics.effect_on_success: Player picks up white orb, gains Purifying Light special action, targets Soul Feast (use TAB), activates skill — entity destroyed.
mechanics.handling: When Soul Feast spawns, a nearby player picks up one of the two white orbs and gains the Purifying Light special action. Target the Soul Feast entity with TAB and activate the skill. Ensure clear line of sight — the boss can body-block the ability. Lingering models after destruction are harmless.

role_tips.dps: If you are near a white orb, pick it up and destroy the Soul Feast. Tab-target it.
role_tips.support: Assign a specific player per pull to handle Soul Feast pickup to avoid confusion.
role_tips.heal: Manage Infirm debuff on the fixated player while Soul Feast is alive.
role_tips.tank: Call out Soul Feast spawns so the assigned player picks up the orb quickly.

mechanics.name: Planet Crash (Defiance Bar)
mechanics.description: Below 50% health, Dagda periodically gains a defiance bar and summons a massive celestial body. The group has a 10-second window to break the bar. Failure causes the celestial body to crash, dealing 90% max HP unavoidable damage to all players. Success causes Dagda to become Exposed, significantly speeding up the fight.
mechanics.trigger: Appears periodically below 50% health.
mechanics.telegraph: Dagda gains a defiance bar; massive celestial body appears above the arena.
mechanics.effect_on_fail: All players take 90% max HP damage — combined with any Infirm stacks this is lethal.
mechanics.effect_on_success: Bar broken; Dagda gains Exposed debuff; lighter AoE spawns instead (safe to ignore).
mechanics.handling: This bar must always be broken. Save major CC skills for Planet Crash. Dump all CC the instant the bar appears. The Exposed buff from a successful break dramatically accelerates the final phases.

role_tips.dps: Save at least one major CC skill for Planet Crash at all times below 50%.
role_tips.support: Coordinate CC with the group. This is the most important breakbar in the instance.
role_tips.heal: Use any CC utilities you have. Prepare emergency healing for a failed bar.
role_tips.tank: Call 'Planet Crash bar — all CC now' immediately.

mechanics.name: Rain of Comets
mechanics.description: A meteor shower covers half the arena with pulsing damage. The visual comet effects themselves do not deal damage — only the ground AoE zones do.
mechanics.trigger: Used occasionally below 50% health.
mechanics.telegraph: Half the arena lights up with comet impact indicators.
mechanics.effect_on_fail: Players standing in the dangerous half take repeated pulsing damage.
mechanics.effect_on_success: Players walk into the safe half of the arena and take no damage.
mechanics.handling: Walk into the safe half immediately when Rain of Comets appears. Can be followed by Demonic Fever — continue managing spread while in the safe half.

role_tips.dps: Walk to safe side. Maintain ranged attacks while moving.
role_tips.support: Move to safe side. Maintain boons.
role_tips.heal: Move to safe side. Continue healing from the safe half.
role_tips.tank: Call 'Rain of Comets — move to safe side' immediately.

timeline_notes: 100-75%: Body-block Shooting Stars constantly. Dodge Spinning Nebula.
Intermissions: Stand between Demonic Blast cones. Kill Kryptis adds quickly. Exit the red pool area fast.
75-50%: Soul Feast begins. Assign someone to pick up white orbs and use Purifying Light.
50%+: Planet Crash bar appears — save CC always. Break it every time.
Final burn: All mechanics layered. Prioritize Planet Crash bar above everything.

pre_pull: When you see 'Beware the Shooting Stars' — if you are the target, move away. Everyone else body-block between Dagda and the target. Need 5 blockers minimum.
Spinning Nebula — back away or dodge immediately.
Spread circles — pick a spot and move, do not overlap.
Intermissions — find a gap between the pizza cones. Kill the Kryptis adds fast. Get out of the red pool.
Soul Feast: pick up the white orb, tab-target the Soul Feast entity, use the Purifying Light special action to destroy it.
Planet Crash bar below 50% — always break it. Save CC.
phase_walkthrough: Phase 1 (100-75%): Shooting Stars body-block every time. Dodge Spinning Nebula. Spread on circles.
Intermission 1 (75%): Avoid Demonic Blast cones. Kill 2 adds. Get away from lethal red pool.
Phase 2 (75-50%): Soul Feast begins — destroy it with Purifying Light.
Intermission 2 (50%): Same as before but 4 adds to kill.
Phase 3 (50-25%): Planet Crash bar — all CC. Rain of Comets — walk to safe side.
Intermission 3 (25%): 6 adds to kill.
Phase 4 (25-0%): Everything active. Break Planet Crash always. Burn Dagda.
recovery_plans: If Planet Crash fires (bar missed), use every defensive cooldown and emergency heal — it deals 90% HP to all.
If Shooting Stars fails and Infirm is applied, tighten body-blocking discipline and top off players immediately.
If adds are slow to die in intermission, move the group to the lethal pool's edge and cleave from maximum safe range.

common_mistakes: Standing still attacking during Shooting Stars instead of moving to body-block.
Not breaking the Planet Crash defiance bar — it deals 90% max HP to all players.
Being slow to exit the lethal red pool during intermission phases.
Spreading circles near the arena edge and being forced into movement that overlaps other players.
Blocking the Purifying Light cast with Dagda's body — ensure line of sight to the Soul Feast entity.
callouts: Shooting Stars — blockers between Dagda and the target, need 5!
Spinning Nebula — back away!
Spread circles — pick a spot and hold it!
Intermission — avoid the cones, kill the adds!
Soul Feast — pick up the orb, tab-target, use Purifying Light!
Planet Crash bar — all CC now, always break it!

name: Temple of Febe

overview.summary: An encounter against Cerus featuring six repeating sin-themed mechanics. Failing mechanics grants Cerus Empowered stacks that directly scale the damage of the final phase. Two split phases at 80% and 50% require defeating exactly two Embodiments from the empowered set. A defiance bar at 80%, 50%, and 10% must be broken to avoid a lethal Petrify cast that also heals Cerus.
overview.goal: Defeat Cerus by managing all six mechanics cleanly, defeating exactly two Embodiments from the empowered set in each split phase, and breaking the defiance bar at every threshold.
wipe_conditions: Defiance bar at 80%, 50%, or 10% is not broken — squad is frozen for 8 seconds and Cerus heals 20% health.
Wail of Despair (red) pools are dropped on top of the boss, blocking navigation for the Envious Gaze wall.
Envious Gaze wall catches the squad on the wrong side, stripping all boons repeatedly.
Two non-empowered Embodiments are killed during a split phase — all three empowered Embodiments are active in the next boss phase.
Empowered stacks exceed ~30 by the final phase, making Enraged Smash unsurvivable.
soft_enrage: Empowered stacks accumulate from every failed mechanic. High stacks make Enraged Smash increasingly lethal.
No hard enrage timer — Cerus has 53 million health, requiring sustained DPS.
overview.hard_enrage: No hard enrage timer. Petrify at breakbar thresholds is the closest equivalent — missing it heals the boss 20% and freezes the squad.

phases.name: Phase 1 – 100% to 80%
phases.description: Cerus cycles through all six sin mechanics: Crushing Regret (greens), Wail of Despair (reds), Envious Gaze (wall), Malicious Intent (adds), Insatiable Hunger (orbs), Cry of Rage (large AoE). Repeat until 80%.

phases.name: Split Phase 1 – 80%
phases.trigger: Cerus reaches 80% health; Petrify defiance bar appears.
phases.description: Break Cerus's defiance bar to trigger the split. Boss separates into six Embodiments — one per sin. Kill exactly two from the empowered set (Eastern set randomized each pull: Rage, Regret, or Envy). Community standard targets Regret and Gluttony. Kill order from empowered set: right Embodiment first, then left, leave center alive.

phases.name: Phase 2 – 80% to 50%
phases.description: Modified mechanic sequence: Regret → Envy → Gluttony → Rage → Malice → Despair. Same mechanics, same handling.

phases.name: Split Phase 2 – 50%
phases.trigger: Cerus reaches 50% health; Petrify defiance bar appears.
phases.description: Break the bar; another split. Same rules — kill exactly two empowered Embodiments. Any failed Embodiment kills from split 1 carry over.

phases.name: Phase 3 – 50% to 10%
phases.description: All mechanics continue. Empowered versions of mechanics from failed splits may be active.

phases.name: Final Phase – 10% Enraged Smash
phases.trigger: Cerus reaches 10% health; Petrify defiance bar appears.
phases.description: Break the 10% bar to trigger Enraged Smash — Cerus smashes the ground every 3 seconds with platform-wide explosions. Severity scales directly with accumulated Empowered stacks. Burn Cerus to zero while surviving Enraged Smash.

roles.dps: Stack in green circles (Regret). Spread reds away from boss and each other (Despair). Dodge or stay outside Rage AoE. Rotate around boss to collect large orbs (Gluttony). Soft-CC Malicious Intent shadows. Rotate past the wall clockwise (Envy).
roles.support: Soft CC for Malicious Intent shadows — immobilize only, no hard CC, knockdowns, or pulls. Provide Stability for Enraged Smash in the final phase. Maintain boon uptime through wall rotations.
roles.heal: Manage sustained damage from all mechanics. Final phase Enraged Smash scales with Empowered — high stacks require dedicated healing focus. Petrify condition from Cerus's tether passively stacks — breaking the bar clears 2 stacks.
roles.tank: No traditional tank. Cerus has no auto-attacks outside the six sin mechanics. Closest player positioning matters only for Malicious Intent shadow baiting.

mechanics.name: Crushing Regret (Greens)
mechanics.description: A large green circle appears on a random player. Five players must be inside the circle at detonation. Failure deals squad-wide damage, applies Expose, and gives Cerus 5 Empowered stacks. Empowered version spawns three smaller circles each requiring 3 players.
mechanics.trigger: Cerus waves arms upward.
mechanics.telegraph: Large green circle on one player.
mechanics.effect_on_fail: Squad-wide damage, Expose, 5 Empowered stacks on Cerus.
mechanics.effect_on_success: Moderate damage shared among 5+ players. No Empowered stacks.
mechanics.handling: Stack tightly near Cerus at all times. When the green circle appears, all non-spread players move onto it. Avoid forced-movement skills while greens are active.

role_tips.dps: Move onto the green circle immediately. Do not use mobility skills that could push you out.
role_tips.support: Stack on the green. Call 'green — stack on it' for new players.
role_tips.heal: Stack on the green. Pre-cast barriers before detonation.
role_tips.tank: Keep the group near Cerus so green stacking is always fast.

mechanics.name: Wail of Despair (Reds)
mechanics.description: A red orb spawns above every player and drops after 5 seconds, creating a persistent pool lasting 60 seconds. Players who remain in pools take sustained damage. Empowered version doubles the pool radius.
mechanics.trigger: Cerus stands ramrod straight; whooshing sound intensifies before drop.
mechanics.telegraph: Red orb above each player; audio cue; Cerus voice lines 'You will suffocate.'
mechanics.effect_on_fail: Reds dropped on or near the boss create persistent pools that block navigation for Envious Gaze and crowd the arena.
mechanics.effect_on_success: Players backstep away from Cerus and drop reds safely away from the boss hitbox; pools land in a clear area.
mechanics.handling: When reds appear, backstep away from Cerus and spread slightly so pools do not overlap. Never drop reds on the boss itself — this blocks wall navigation. Pools last 60 seconds and clutch the arena if poorly placed.

role_tips.dps: Backstep away from Cerus immediately. Move far enough that your pool does not overlap others.
role_tips.support: Call 'reds — backstep' and model correct behavior.
role_tips.heal: Backstep. Heal anyone who clips the pool after detonation.
role_tips.tank: Call out red timing from audio cues. Remind group never to drop on the boss.

mechanics.name: Envious Gaze (Wall)
mechanics.description: Cerus turns to face a random player. An arrow telegraph points in the wall's direction. A massive wall spawns and rotates counterclockwise, corrupting 3 boons per tick and dealing moderate damage to players it hits. The boss's hitbox center is safe — the wall does not extend into it.
mechanics.trigger: Cerus turns and faces a random player; arrow telegraph appears.
mechanics.telegraph: Arrow indicator on Cerus pointing direction of wall; wall visibly materializes and begins rotating.
mechanics.effect_on_fail: Players caught by the wall lose boons continuously and take sustained damage. Empowered version adds a second wall.
mechanics.effect_on_success: Players strafe past the clockwise side of the arrow indicator and rotate around the boss in lockstep with the wall.
mechanics.handling: When the wall spawns, move to the clockwise side of the arrow indicator and rotate around Cerus in the same direction as the rotation (counterclockwise wall means move counterclockwise to stay just ahead of it). If caught on the wrong side, dodge through the boss's center hitbox. Empowered: a second arrow spawns half a rotation away — move past the counterclockwise side of the player arrow, then past the slower wall's starting point.

role_tips.dps: Watch the arrow. Move counterclockwise around the boss to stay ahead of the wall.
role_tips.support: Keep the group moving together. Call rotation direction if needed.
role_tips.heal: Rotate with the group. Do not stop to heal during the wall rotation.
role_tips.tank: Call 'wall rotating — keep moving counterclockwise' so the group stays together.

mechanics.name: Malicious Intent (Malice Shadow)
mechanics.description: A demonic tether attaches to a random player with a 5-second timer. When the timer expires, a Malicious Shadow spawns at the player's location and walks toward Cerus. If it reaches the boss, Cerus gains 5 Empowered stacks and heals. Soft CC (Immobilize, Cripple, Chill — not hard CC, knockdowns, pulls, or fear) stops the shadow. Empowered version targets three players for 15 potential stacks.
mechanics.trigger: Cerus slams the ground with all limbs; timer above player's head.
mechanics.telegraph: Demonic tether on player; timer fills above head; screen message 'Cerus is drawing out your malice'; pulsating sound intensifies.
mechanics.effect_on_fail: Shadow reaches Cerus — 5 Empowered stacks and boss heals. Empowered: 15 stacks.
mechanics.effect_on_success: Shadow is immobilized by soft CC before reaching Cerus; group cleaves it down.
mechanics.handling: If you have the tether, dodge backward to spawn the shadow away from Cerus. Organized groups use soft CC (Immobilize) on the shadow near the boss so it can be cleaved simultaneously with boss damage. Use ONLY soft CC — hard CC, knockdowns, pulls, and fears do not work and waste cooldowns.

role_tips.dps: Use Immobilize or Cripple on the shadow when it spawns. Do not use knockbacks or pulls.
role_tips.support: Apply soft CC to the shadow. Coordinate with other support players.
role_tips.heal: Soft CC the shadow if you have it. Avoid accidental hard CC.
role_tips.tank: Call 'malice — soft CC only' before the pull as a reminder.

mechanics.name: Insatiable Hunger (Gluttony Orbs)
mechanics.description: Demonic orbs spawn at the arena edge. Small orbs can be destroyed or reflected (which heals Cerus — do not reflect). Three large orbs only disappear when a player walks through them; each grants Cerus 1 Empowered stack and applies 30 seconds of Torment (350 damage/second per stack) to the collecting player. All three large orbs must be collected. Empowered version spawns five large orbs lasting 3 minutes of Torment.
mechanics.trigger: Cerus props on appendages, eating from hands.
mechanics.telegraph: Orbs visibly spawn at platform edge; Cerus voice lines 'I feed…'
mechanics.effect_on_fail: Large orbs reach Cerus — each applies 1 Empowered stack.
mechanics.effect_on_success: All three large orbs are collected by players rotating around the boss. Torment is distributed across multiple players.
mechanics.handling: Rotate around the boss and walk through all three large orbs to collect them. Place reflect or absorb abilities on Cerus as small orbs approach — this prevents small orbs from healing Cerus. Distribute large orb collection so no single player takes more than 2-3 (especially important in the Empowered 5-orb version).

role_tips.dps: Rotate around the boss to collect large orbs. Do not all converge on the same orb.
role_tips.support: Place absorb abilities on Cerus for small orbs. Help collect large orbs if nearby.
role_tips.heal: Manage Torment damage on orb collectors. Cleanse where possible.
role_tips.tank: Call orb positions to help the group collect all three (or five in Empowered).

mechanics.name: Cry of Rage (Rage AoE)
mechanics.description: A large expanding AoE appears at Cerus's feet and detonates after about 5 seconds, dealing massive damage and applying Expose. Empowered version is undodgeable — the AoE becomes large enough that it cannot be outrun with a single dodge.
mechanics.trigger: Cerus cries out and drums his appendages.
mechanics.telegraph: Large expanding AoE indicator at Cerus's feet.
mechanics.effect_on_fail: Hit by the explosion — high damage and Expose applied.
mechanics.effect_on_success: Dodge timed correctly as the AoE fills; or stand outside the AoE range.
mechanics.handling: Dodge as the AoE indicator fills, not at the start. High-ping players should move slightly earlier. Empowered: the AoE is undodgeable — the group must preemptively move outside the range entirely, or use invulnerability sources.

role_tips.dps: Dodge the AoE as it fills. Do not dodge at the start of the cast.
role_tips.support: Dodge or position outside the AoE. Call Empowered Rage early so the group repositions.
role_tips.heal: Dodge or move out. After detonation, cleanse Expose from anyone caught.
role_tips.tank: Call 'Rage AoE — prepare to dodge' so the group times it correctly.

mechanics.name: Petrify (Defiance Bar)
mechanics.description: At 80%, 50%, and 10% health, Cerus channels Petrify. If the full cast completes, the squad is frozen for 8 seconds, takes high damage, and Cerus heals 20% of his maximum health (affected by Poison). Breaking the bar interrupts the cast and transitions the phase. Cerus also passively applies Petrify stacks via his tether throughout the fight, reducing movement, damage, and skill speed — breaking the bar removes 2 stacks immediately.
mechanics.trigger: Cerus reaches 80%, 50%, and 10% health thresholds.
mechanics.telegraph: Blue defiance bar appears; Cerus channels Petrify.
mechanics.effect_on_fail: Squad frozen for 8 seconds; high damage; Cerus heals 20% health. Healing can be partially mitigated by Poison.
mechanics.effect_on_success: Bar broken; Petrify cast interrupted; 2 Petrify stacks removed; phase transitions.
mechanics.handling: Save major CC skills for each bar. Dump all CC the instant the bar appears. Coordinate CC to break it as fast as possible — the 20% heal on failure can extend the fight significantly.

role_tips.dps: Always have CC available for the defiance bar. Do not blow CC skills on non-critical moments.
role_tips.support: Coordinate CC timing. Call 'bar up — CC now.'
role_tips.heal: Apply Poison to Cerus before a potential Petrify failure to reduce the heal.
role_tips.tank: Call each bar immediately and lead the CC dump.

mechanics.name: Split Phase – Embodiments
mechanics.description: After breaking the Petrify bar at 80% and 50%, Cerus splits into six Embodiments (one per sin mechanic). An Eastern set (randomly empowered each pull: Rage, Regret, or Envy) and Western set (Malice, Gluttony, Despair). Kill exactly two Embodiments from the empowered set and leave all others alive. Killing non-empowered Embodiments causes all three empowered Embodiments to be active in the next boss phase.
mechanics.trigger: Petrify defiance bar broken at 80% and 50%.
mechanics.telegraph: Cerus splits into six distinct Embodiment figures in the arena.
mechanics.effect_on_fail: Wrong Embodiments killed — all three empowered Embodiments active next phase, stacking multiple Empowered mechanics simultaneously.
mechanics.effect_on_success: Exactly two empowered Embodiments killed — only one empowered mechanic active next phase (from the surviving center Embodiment).
mechanics.handling: Community standard: target Regret and Gluttony (lowest fail impact). Kill the rightmost Embodiment of the empowered set first, then the leftmost, and leave the center alive. Each Embodiment uses its own mechanic without voice line warnings — watch the arena constantly during splits. Drop Wail of Despair (red) pools away from other Embodiments.

role_tips.dps: Focus the assigned Embodiment targets only. Do not AoE into non-target Embodiments accidentally.
role_tips.support: Call which Embodiments to kill. Watch the non-target side for dangerous mechanics.
role_tips.heal: Keep the group alive during the split — Embodiments use mechanics without voice line warnings.
role_tips.tank: Assign Embodiment targets before the pull and reconfirm before each split phase.

mechanics.name: Enraged Smash (Final Phase)
mechanics.description: Triggered by breaking the 10% defiance bar. Cerus smashes the ground every 3 seconds, generating platform-wide demonic energy expulsions. Damage scales directly with accumulated Empowered stacks — below 30 stacks is manageable with standard healing; above 30 stacks requires dedicated emergency healing.
mechanics.trigger: Breaking the 10% Petrify defiance bar.
mechanics.telegraph: Cerus begins ground smashing; energy eruptions on platform.
mechanics.effect_on_fail: Players fail to block, dodge, or use invulnerability — take repeated heavy hits that scale with Empowered stacks.
mechanics.effect_on_success: Players cycle blocks, dodges, and invulnerability sources; healers manage the sustained damage. Cerus dies before Empowered stacks make it unrecoverable.
mechanics.handling: Block, dodge, or use invulnerability for each smash. Cycle defensive cooldowns. Burn Cerus as fast as possible — the fight ends when he dies.

role_tips.dps: Cycle blocks and dodges. Burn Cerus — the faster he dies, the fewer smashes you take.
role_tips.support: Provide Stability to prevent knockbacks from smashes. Maintain boons during the burn.
role_tips.heal: This is the healing check. Stack Empowered stacks below 30 to keep it manageable. Use major healing cooldowns.
role_tips.tank: Call 'Enraged Smash — block, dodge, invuln cycle' so everyone rotates defensives.

timeline_notes: Phase 1 (100-80%): Learn all six mechanics. Stack greens, backstep reds away from boss, rotate for wall, soft-CC shadows, collect orbs, dodge Rage.
80% bar: Break it. Split phase — kill exactly two empowered Embodiments.
Phase 2 (80-50%): Modified mechanic order. Empowered versions may appear from failed mechanics.
50% bar: Break it. Second split phase — kill exactly two empowered Embodiments.
Phase 3 (50-10%): All mechanics plus Empowered versions from split failures.
10% bar: Break it — triggers Enraged Smash. Burn Cerus. Scale of final phase determined by total Empowered stacks accumulated.

pre_pull: Six mechanics repeat throughout the fight. Each failure gives Cerus Empowered stacks that make the final phase harder.
Greens (Regret): stack on the circle — need 5 players inside.
Reds (Despair): backstep away from Cerus, spread, never drop red pools on the boss.
Wall (Envy): rotate counterclockwise around Cerus to stay ahead of the wall.
Shadows (Malice): soft CC only — Immobilize, Cripple, Chill. No knockbacks, no pulls, no fears.
Orbs (Gluttony): walk through the three large orbs to collect them. Do not reflect small orbs.
Rage: dodge as the AoE fills, not at the start.
Defiance bars at 80%, 50%, 10%: always break them — failing heals Cerus 20%.
Split phases: kill exactly two empowered Embodiments. Do not accidentally kill non-empowered ones.
phase_walkthrough: Phase 1 (100-80%): All six mechanics in rotation. Handle each cleanly.
80% bar: Break it → split. Kill right empowered first, left second, leave center alive.
Phase 2 (80-50%): Modified order. Empowered versions possible. Continue clean mechanic execution.
50% bar: Break it → split. Same Embodiment kill priority.
Phase 3 (50-10%): All mechanics. Avoid letting Empowered stacks climb above 30.
10% bar: Break it → Enraged Smash. Cycle blocks, dodges, invulns. Burn Cerus to zero.
recovery_plans: If the Petrify bar is missed, Cerus heals 20% — apply Poison before a potential failure to reduce the heal. Accept the setback and continue.
If wrong Embodiments are killed in a split phase, three empowered Embodiment mechanics will be active next phase — use all defensive resources and prioritize mechanic execution over DPS.
If Empowered stacks are above 30 going into Enraged Smash, maximize defensive cooldown cycling and emergency healing.

common_mistakes: Dropping red pools on or near the boss, blocking wall rotation paths.
Using hard CC on Malicious Intent shadows instead of soft CC — shadows are immune to hard CC.
Killing non-empowered Embodiments during split phases, causing three active empowered mechanics next phase.
Not breaking the Petrify defiance bar at 80%, 50%, or 10%, allowing Cerus to heal 20%.
Accumulated Empowered stacks above 30 making Enraged Smash unmanageable in the final phase.
callouts: Greens — stack on the circle, need 5!
Reds — backstep away from Cerus, spread out!
Wall rotating — move counterclockwise!
Shadow spawning — soft CC only, Immobilize it!
Orbs — rotate and collect the three large ones!
Rage AoE — dodge as it fills!
Bar at 80/50/10% — all CC now, break it!
Split phase — kill right then left empowered, leave center alive!
